montana.name: Montana

statePrograms.name: MBOH Bond Advantage Program
statePrograms.description: Below-market interest rate mortgages with optional down payment assistance for qualifying first-time homebuyers in Montana.

montana.metaTitle: Montana Mortgage Calculator 2026 | MT Home Loan Payment Estimator
montana.metaDescription: Calculate your Montana mortgage payment. Montana median home price ~$430K, no sales tax, low property taxes (0.84%). Bozeman market has cooled from pandemic peak. Free MT calculator 2026.
montana.intro: Montana experienced one of the most dramatic housing booms of the pandemic era, particularly in Bozeman, which saw prices surge as remote workers flooded in from California and other high-cost states. The market has since cooled from its peak. Montana has no state sales tax, which reduces the overall cost of living.
montana.taxContent: Montana's 0.84% property tax rate is below the national average. The state has no sales tax, a significant financial benefit. Montana's income tax tops out at 6.75%. Property taxes vary by county, with Gallatin County (Bozeman) having higher rates due to strong demand.
montana.insuranceContent: Montana faces significant wildfire risk, particularly in western Montana (Missoula, Kalispell, Flathead Valley). Wildfire insurance costs have increased substantially in recent years. Eastern Montana has more moderate rates. Budget $900–$2,000+/year depending on location and wildfire risk.
montana.marketContent: Bozeman has become one of the most expensive small cities in the Mountain West, driven by Montana State University, tech sector growth, and lifestyle demand. Missoula (University of Montana) has also seen strong appreciation. Billings offers more affordable options with a stable energy and agriculture economy. Much of rural Montana qualifies for USDA loans.

nebraska.name: Nebraska

statePrograms.name: NIFA Homebuyer Assistance Program
statePrograms.description: Below-market interest rate mortgages with optional down payment assistance for qualifying first-time homebuyers in Nebraska.

nebraska.metaTitle: Nebraska Mortgage Calculator 2026 | NE Home Loan Payment Estimator
nebraska.metaDescription: Calculate your Nebraska mortgage payment. Nebraska median home price ~$250K but above-average property taxes (1.73%). Omaha is a major financial hub. Free NE calculator 2026.
nebraska.intro: Nebraska offers affordable home prices but above-average property taxes (1.73%), which is an important factor in calculating your total monthly payment. Omaha has emerged as a significant financial and insurance hub — home to Berkshire Hathaway and Mutual of Omaha — providing stable, high-quality employment.
nebraska.taxContent: Nebraska's 1.73% property tax rate is significantly above the national average — on a $250,000 home, about $360/month. Nebraska offers a Homestead Exemption for qualifying seniors, veterans, and disabled individuals. The state income tax tops out at 5.84% (being reduced over time).
nebraska.insuranceContent: Nebraska is in Tornado Alley with significant severe weather risk including tornadoes, hail, and severe thunderstorms. Annual insurance averages $1,600–$2,200. The 2019 flooding along the Missouri and Platte rivers highlighted flood risk in some areas. Budget $135–$185/month.
nebraska.marketContent: Omaha has a strong, stable economy driven by financial services, insurance, and healthcare. The metro offers excellent value with strong schools and amenities. Lincoln has a stable university-driven market. Much of rural Nebraska qualifies for USDA loans, making zero-down homeownership accessible across the state.

nevada.name: Nevada

statePrograms.name: Home Is Possible Program
statePrograms.description: Down payment assistance of up to 4% of the loan amount for qualifying homebuyers in Nevada.

nevada.metaTitle: Nevada Mortgage Calculator 2026 | NV Home Loan Payment Estimator
nevada.metaDescription: Calculate your Nevada mortgage payment. Nevada has no income tax and low property taxes (0.60%). Las Vegas market has cooled from 2022 peak. Community property state. Free NV calculator 2026.
nevada.intro: Nevada's combination of no state income tax, low property taxes (0.60%), and no inheritance tax makes it one of the most tax-friendly states for homeowners. Las Vegas and Reno experienced dramatic price appreciation during 2020–2022 before cooling. Nevada is a community property state.
nevada.taxContent: Nevada has no state income tax. Property taxes are low at 0.60%, and Nevada's abatement law caps annual increases at 3% for primary residences. On a $420,000 home, expect ~$210/month in taxes. Nevada is a community property state.
nevada.insuranceContent: Nevada's desert climate creates specific insurance considerations. Wildfire risk is significant in the Reno/Tahoe area. Las Vegas has lower wildfire risk. Standard insurance averages $900–$1,400/year. Budget $75–$120/month.
nevada.marketContent: Las Vegas saw extraordinary appreciation during 2020–2022 as California residents relocated, then corrected with rising inventory. Henderson and North Las Vegas offer more affordable options. Reno has seen strong growth driven by tech companies (Tesla Gigafactory, Apple, Google). Lake Tahoe commands premium prices as a resort market.

new-hampshire.name: New Hampshire

statePrograms.name: NHHFA Home Flex Program
statePrograms.description: Below-market interest rate mortgages with optional down payment assistance for qualifying homebuyers in New Hampshire.

new-hampshire.metaTitle: New Hampshire Mortgage Calculator 2026 | NH Home Loan Payment Estimator
new-hampshire.metaDescription: Calculate your New Hampshire mortgage payment. NH has no income tax but very high property taxes (avg 2.18%). Popular with Boston-area buyers. Free NH calculator 2026.
new-hampshire.intro: New Hampshire presents a unique tax paradox: no state income tax and no sales tax, but property taxes averaging 2.18% — among the highest in the nation. The state funds most public services through property taxes, so the income tax savings are largely offset by higher property tax bills.
new-hampshire.taxContent: New Hampshire has no state income tax on wages and no sales tax. However, property taxes average 2.18% — on a $430,000 home, that's ~$781/month in taxes alone. This is a critical factor many buyers underestimate. Rates vary significantly by municipality, with some towns above 3%.
new-hampshire.insuranceContent: Insurance costs are moderate at $900–$1,300/year. The state faces winter storm risk including heavy snowfall, ice storms, and nor'easters. Coastal properties (Hampton Beach, Seabrook) face some flood and storm surge risk. Budget $75–$110/month.
new-hampshire.marketContent: Southern New Hampshire (Manchester, Nashua, Derry, Salem) is within commuting distance of Boston and has seen strong demand and appreciation. The Lakes Region and White Mountains are premium second-home markets. Concord offers more affordable options. Tight inventory and strong Massachusetts buyer demand keep prices elevated.

new-jersey.name: New Jersey

statePrograms.name: NJHMFA Down Payment Assistance
statePrograms.agency: New Jersey Housing and Mortgage Finance Agency (NJHMFA)
statePrograms.description: Down payment assistance of up to $15,000 as a 0% interest, 5-year forgivable loan for qualifying first-time buyers.

new-jersey.metaTitle: New Jersey Mortgage Calculator 2026 | NJ Home Loan Payment Estimator
new-jersey.metaDescription: Calculate your New Jersey mortgage payment. NJ has the highest property taxes in the nation (avg 2.23%) — a critical factor in your monthly payment. High conforming loan limits. Free NJ calculator 2026.
new-jersey.intro: New Jersey has the highest effective property tax rate in the United States at 2.23%, which is the single most important factor for buyers to understand. On a $490,000 home, property taxes alone add approximately $911 per month to your payment. Despite this, NJ's proximity to NYC and Philadelphia and excellent schools continue to drive demand.
new-jersey.taxContent: New Jersey's 2.23% average property tax rate means a $490,000 home costs ~$911/month in taxes alone. Some Bergen, Essex, and Union County towns have effective rates above 3%. New Jersey offers a Homestead Benefit for qualifying homeowners. The state income tax tops out at 10.75%.
new-jersey.insuranceContent: Insurance costs are moderate at $1,100–$1,500/year. Coastal areas (Jersey Shore) face flood and hurricane risk — Hurricane Sandy (2012) caused catastrophic damage. Flood insurance is mandatory in FEMA flood zones. Budget $90–$130/month plus flood insurance if applicable.
new-jersey.marketContent: Hudson County (Jersey City, Hoboken) has seen dramatic appreciation as NYC buyers seek more space. Bergen, Essex, and Morris counties offer premium suburban living with NYC commuting access. The Jersey Shore is a premium second-home market. South Jersey offers more affordable options with Philadelphia access. NJ's $1,149,825 conforming limit is essential for NYC metro buyers.

new-mexico.name: New Mexico

statePrograms.name: MFA FirstHome Program
statePrograms.description: Below-market interest rate mortgages with optional down payment assistance for qualifying first-time homebuyers in New Mexico.

new-mexico.metaTitle: New Mexico Mortgage Calculator 2026 | NM Home Loan Payment Estimator
new-mexico.metaDescription: Calculate your New Mexico mortgage payment. New Mexico median home price ~$290K, low property taxes (0.80%), community property state. Albuquerque and Santa Fe are distinct markets. Free NM calculator 2026.
new-mexico.intro: New Mexico offers affordable home prices relative to its Mountain West neighbors, with a median around $290,000 and low property taxes. The state has two distinct housing markets: Albuquerque (driven by Kirtland AFB and Sandia National Laboratories) and Santa Fe (a premium arts and culture destination). New Mexico is a community property state.
new-mexico.taxContent: New Mexico's 0.80% property tax rate is below the national average. New Mexico is a community property state, meaning assets acquired during marriage are generally owned equally by both spouses. The state income tax tops out at 5.9%.
new-mexico.insuranceContent: New Mexico faces wildfire risk, particularly in the northern mountains. The 2022 Hermits Peak/Calf Canyon Fire was the largest in state history. Insurance costs have increased in high-risk areas. Albuquerque averages $1,200–$1,600/year. Budget $100–$140/month.
new-mexico.marketContent: Albuquerque has a stable economy driven by Kirtland AFB, Sandia National Laboratories, Intel, and healthcare. Santa Fe is a premium market driven by arts, culture, and second-home demand with medians significantly above the state average. Rio Rancho (Albuquerque suburb) has seen strong growth. Much of rural New Mexico qualifies for USDA loans.

new-york.name: New York

statePrograms.name: SONYMA Achieving the Dream
statePrograms.agency: State of New York Mortgage Agency (SONYMA)
statePrograms.description: Below-market interest rate mortgages with down payment assistance for qualifying first-time homebuyers in New York.

new-york.metaTitle: New York Mortgage Calculator 2026 | NY Home Loan Payment Estimator
new-york.metaDescription: Calculate your New York mortgage payment. NY has high conforming loan limits ($1,149,825) and high property taxes (avg 1.72%). NYC HomeFirst offers up to $100K in DPA. Free NY calculator 2026.
new-york.intro: New York State encompasses some of the most expensive real estate in the world (Manhattan) and some of the most affordable (upstate cities like Buffalo and Syracuse). The state has high conforming loan limits ($1,149,825) in the NYC metro area. NYC's HomeFirst program offers up to $100,000 in down payment assistance for qualifying buyers.
new-york.taxContent: New York's property tax averages 1.72% statewide but varies enormously by location. NYC has a complex system with often lower effective rates for residential properties. Westchester, Nassau, and Suffolk counties have some of the highest property taxes in the nation. State income tax tops out at 10.9%, plus NYC's own tax of up to 3.876%.
new-york.insuranceContent: NYC properties (condos and co-ops) have different insurance structures than single-family homes. Long Island and coastal areas face flood and hurricane risk — Hurricane Sandy (2012) caused catastrophic damage. Flood insurance is mandatory in FEMA flood zones. Budget $100–$140/month, higher in flood-prone areas.
new-york.marketContent: Manhattan and Brooklyn command some of the highest prices globally. The outer boroughs offer more affordable options within NYC. Westchester, Nassau, and Suffolk counties are premium suburban markets. Upstate cities (Buffalo, Rochester, Syracuse) offer dramatically more affordable housing. The Hudson Valley has seen strong demand from NYC buyers. NY's $1,149,825 conforming limit is essential for NYC metro buyers.

north-carolina.name: North Carolina

statePrograms.name: NC Home Advantage Mortgage
statePrograms.description: Down payment assistance of up to 3% of the loan amount for qualifying first-time and move-up buyers in North Carolina.

north-carolina.metaTitle: North Carolina Mortgage Calculator 2026 | NC Home Loan Payment Estimator
north-carolina.metaDescription: Calculate your North Carolina mortgage payment. NC median home price ~$330K, low property taxes (0.82%). Charlotte and Raleigh-Durham are among the Southeast's fastest-growing metros. Free NC calculator 2026.
north-carolina.intro: North Carolina has emerged as one of the most dynamic states for housing demand, driven by the explosive growth of Charlotte (banking and finance) and the Research Triangle (Raleigh-Durham-Chapel Hill, home to major tech and pharmaceutical companies). Low property taxes and a declining flat income tax make NC increasingly competitive.
north-carolina.taxContent: North Carolina's 0.82% property tax rate is below the national average. The state offers an Elderly/Disabled Exclusion for qualifying homeowners. North Carolina's income tax is a flat 4.5% (being reduced over time), making it increasingly competitive.
north-carolina.insuranceContent: North Carolina faces hurricane risk along its coast (Outer Banks, Wilmington, New Bern). Inland areas have more moderate costs. The state also faces tornado risk in the Piedmont region. Annual insurance ranges from $1,400–$2,500 coastal to $1,200–$1,800 inland. Budget $100–$210/month.
north-carolina.marketContent: Charlotte has become a major banking and financial services hub (Bank of America, Wells Fargo), driving strong job growth. The Research Triangle has attracted major tech and pharma companies (Apple, Google, Pfizer, Biogen). Both metros remain more affordable than comparable coastal cities. Fort Liberty (formerly Fort Bragg) and Camp Lejeune support significant military housing demand.

north-dakota.name: North Dakota

statePrograms.name: NDHFA FirstHome Program
statePrograms.description: Below-market interest rate mortgages with optional down payment assistance for qualifying first-time homebuyers in North Dakota.

north-dakota.metaTitle: North Dakota Mortgage Calculator 2026 | ND Home Loan Payment Estimator
north-dakota.metaDescription: Calculate your North Dakota mortgage payment. North Dakota median home price ~$250K, low property taxes (0.98%), very low income tax (top rate 2.5%). Free ND calculator 2026.
north-dakota.intro: North Dakota offers affordable homeownership with a median price around $250,000 and a very low income tax rate (top rate 2.5% — one of the lowest in the nation). Fargo has emerged as a growing tech and healthcare hub, while the state's oil and gas industry drives economic activity in the west.
north-dakota.taxContent: North Dakota's 0.98% property tax rate is close to the national average. The state offers a Homestead Credit for qualifying seniors and disabled individuals. North Dakota's income tax tops out at just 2.5% — one of the lowest rates in the nation.
north-dakota.insuranceContent: North Dakota faces significant winter storm risk with heavy snowfall, blizzards, and extreme cold. Flooding is a concern in the Red River Valley (Fargo area). Annual insurance averages $1,200–$1,600. Budget $100–$135/month.
north-dakota.marketContent: Fargo has a growing economy driven by healthcare, technology, and agriculture. Bismarck (state capital) has a stable government and energy-driven market. Minot (home to Minot AFB) has a military-influenced market. Western North Dakota (Williston, Dickinson) has seen boom-and-bust cycles tied to oil prices. Much of rural North Dakota qualifies for USDA loans.

ohio.name: Ohio

statePrograms.name: OHFA Your Choice! Down Payment Assistance
statePrograms.description: Down payment assistance of 2.5% or 5% of the purchase price for qualifying homebuyers in Ohio.

ohio.metaTitle: Ohio Mortgage Calculator 2026 | OH Home Loan Payment Estimator
ohio.metaDescription: Calculate your Ohio mortgage payment. Ohio median home price ~$230K but above-average property taxes (1.59%). Columbus is one of the Midwest's fastest-growing cities. Free OH calculator 2026.
ohio.intro: Ohio offers affordable home prices but above-average property taxes (1.59%), creating a nuanced affordability picture. Columbus has emerged as one of the fastest-growing major cities in the Midwest, driven by Ohio State University, a growing tech sector, and Intel's massive semiconductor plant.
ohio.taxContent: Ohio's 1.59% property tax rate is above the national average — on a $230,000 home, about $305/month. Ohio offers a Homestead Exemption for qualifying seniors and disabled homeowners. The state income tax tops out at 3.99% (being reduced over time).
ohio.insuranceContent: Ohio faces tornado risk, particularly in the western part of the state. Severe thunderstorms and occasional flooding are also concerns. Annual insurance averages $1,100–$1,500. Budget $90–$130/month.
ohio.marketContent: Columbus has been one of the fastest-growing Midwest cities, driven by Ohio State University, Intel's semiconductor plant, and a growing tech sector. Cleveland has seen revitalization in Ohio City and Tremont with very affordable prices. Cincinnati offers strong value with a diverse economy (P&G, Kroger). Much of rural Ohio qualifies for USDA loans.

oklahoma.name: Oklahoma

statePrograms.name: OHFA Dream Program
statePrograms.description: Down payment assistance of 3.5% of the loan amount for qualifying first-time homebuyers in Oklahoma.

oklahoma.metaTitle: Oklahoma Mortgage Calculator 2026 | OK Home Loan Payment Estimator
oklahoma.metaDescription: Calculate your Oklahoma mortgage payment. Oklahoma has very affordable home prices (~$210K) but the highest homeowners insurance costs in the nation due to tornado risk ($250–$420/mo). Free OK calculator 2026.
oklahoma.intro: Oklahoma offers some of the most affordable home prices in the nation, but buyers must carefully account for homeowners insurance costs — Oklahoma has the highest insurance premiums in the United States due to its location in the heart of Tornado Alley. Annual costs of $3,500–$5,000+ are common, adding $290–$420 per month to your housing costs.
oklahoma.taxContent: Oklahoma's 0.90% property tax rate is close to the national average. The Homestead Exemption reduces assessed value by $1,000 for primary residences. Oklahoma's income tax tops out at 4.75%. The combination of affordable home prices and moderate taxes makes Oklahoma very affordable — if you can manage the insurance costs.
oklahoma.insuranceContent: Oklahoma has the highest homeowners insurance costs in the nation due to Tornado Alley location. Annual premiums average $3,500–$5,000, with some areas seeing $6,000+. Hail damage is extremely common. Budget $290–$420+/month for homeowners insurance — this dramatically affects your total monthly payment.
oklahoma.marketContent: Oklahoma City has a diverse economy spanning energy, aerospace, and healthcare with strong job growth and very affordable prices. Edmond and Norman (University of Oklahoma) are popular suburbs. Tulsa has a growing arts and tech scene with very affordable prices. The state has significant military presence (Tinker AFB, Fort Sill, Vance AFB), supporting VA loan demand.

oregon.name: Oregon

statePrograms.name: Oregon Bond Residential Loan Program
statePrograms.description: Below-market interest rate mortgages with optional down payment assistance for qualifying first-time homebuyers in Oregon.

oregon.metaTitle: Oregon Mortgage Calculator 2026 | OR Home Loan Payment Estimator
oregon.metaDescription: Calculate your Oregon mortgage payment. Oregon median home price ~$490K, no sales tax, high income taxes. Portland market has cooled from 2022 peak. Earthquake risk. Free OR calculator 2026.
oregon.intro: Oregon has no state sales tax, which reduces the cost of living, but the state has high income taxes (up to 9.9%) that significantly affect overall affordability. Portland experienced dramatic price appreciation during 2020–2022 before cooling. Oregon faces significant earthquake risk from the Cascadia Subduction Zone.
oregon.taxContent: Oregon's 0.97% property tax rate is close to the national average. Oregon has no state sales tax — a significant financial benefit. However, the state income tax tops out at 9.9% for high earners, among the highest in the nation. Measure 5 caps property taxes at $5 per $1,000 of real market value for education.
oregon.insuranceContent: Oregon faces significant earthquake risk from the Cascadia Subduction Zone — separate earthquake insurance averages $800–$2,500/year. Wildfire risk is significant in eastern Oregon and the Cascade foothills. Standard insurance averages $900–$1,400/year. Budget $900–$1,400/year plus earthquake coverage.
oregon.marketContent: Portland has more inventory and longer days on market than during the 2022 peak, offering buyers more options. The tech sector (Intel, Nike, Adidas) drives demand in west side suburbs (Hillsboro, Beaverton). Salem and Eugene offer more affordable alternatives. Bend is a premium lifestyle market. Much of rural Oregon qualifies for USDA loans.

pennsylvania.name: Pennsylvania

statePrograms.name: PHFA Keystone Home Loan
statePrograms.description: Below-market interest rate mortgages with optional down payment assistance for qualifying homebuyers in Pennsylvania.

pennsylvania.metaTitle: Pennsylvania Mortgage Calculator 2026 | PA Home Loan Payment Estimator
pennsylvania.metaDescription: Calculate your Pennsylvania mortgage payment. PA median home price ~$280K but above-average property taxes (1.58%). Philadelphia and Pittsburgh are major economic hubs. Free PA calculator 2026.
pennsylvania.intro: Pennsylvania offers relatively affordable home prices compared to its Mid-Atlantic neighbors, but above-average property taxes (1.58%) are an important factor in calculating your total monthly payment. Philadelphia and Pittsburgh are both undergoing significant revitalization, attracting young professionals and corporate investment.
pennsylvania.taxContent: Pennsylvania's 1.58% property tax rate is above the national average — on a $280,000 home, about $369/month. The Homestead Exclusion reduces assessed value for primary residences. Pennsylvania's income tax is a flat 3.07% — one of the lowest flat rates in the nation.
pennsylvania.insuranceContent: Pennsylvania's insurance costs are moderate at $1,000–$1,400/year. Flooding is a concern in some areas, particularly along the Susquehanna River and in low-lying communities. Budget $85–$120/month.
pennsylvania.marketContent: Philadelphia has seen strong revitalization in neighborhoods like Fishtown, Northern Liberties, and South Philly, with prices well below comparable NYC neighborhoods. Pittsburgh has transformed from a steel city to a tech and healthcare hub (Carnegie Mellon, University of Pittsburgh). The Lehigh Valley (Allentown, Bethlehem) offers affordable options with NYC/Philadelphia access. Much of rural Pennsylvania qualifies for USDA loans.

rhode-island.name: Rhode Island

statePrograms.name: RIHousing FirstHomes Program
statePrograms.description: Below-market interest rate mortgages with optional down payment assistance for qualifying first-time homebuyers in Rhode Island.

rhode-island.metaTitle: Rhode Island Mortgage Calculator 2026 | RI Home Loan Payment Estimator
rhode-island.metaDescription: Calculate your Rhode Island mortgage payment. Rhode Island median home price ~$430K with above-average property taxes (1.63%). Providence is a growing arts and tech hub. Free RI calculator 2026.
rhode-island.intro: Rhode Island is the smallest state but has a surprisingly dynamic housing market, driven by Providence's growing arts, tech, and healthcare sectors and its proximity to Boston. Above-average property taxes (1.63%) are an important factor in calculating your total monthly payment.
rhode-island.taxContent: Rhode Island's 1.63% property tax rate is above the national average — on a $430,000 home, about $585/month. Rates vary significantly by municipality. Rhode Island's income tax tops out at 5.99%. The state has been working to improve its tax competitiveness.
rhode-island.insuranceContent: Rhode Island's coastal location creates flood and hurricane risk, particularly in coastal communities. Flood insurance is required in FEMA flood zones. Standard insurance averages $1,100–$1,500/year. Budget $90–$130/month.
rhode-island.marketContent: Providence has emerged as a growing hub for arts, tech, and healthcare, attracting young professionals priced out of Boston. Newport is a premium coastal market. The state's proximity to Boston (about 1 hour) makes it attractive for buyers seeking lower prices with Boston access. Tight inventory has kept prices elevated.

south-carolina.name: South Carolina

statePrograms.name: SC Housing Homebuyer Program
statePrograms.agency: South Carolina State Housing Finance and Development Authority
statePrograms.description: Below-market interest rate mortgages with optional down payment assistance for qualifying first-time homebuyers in South Carolina.

south-carolina.metaTitle: South Carolina Mortgage Calculator 2026 | SC Home Loan Payment Estimator
south-carolina.metaDescription: Calculate your South Carolina mortgage payment. SC median home price ~$290K, low property taxes (0.57%), but higher insurance costs due to hurricane risk. Free SC calculator 2026.
south-carolina.intro: South Carolina offers affordable home prices and low property taxes (0.57%), making it one of the more cost-effective states for homeownership. Charleston has emerged as one of the most desirable cities in the Southeast, while Greenville has seen strong growth driven by manufacturing and tech.
south-carolina.taxContent: South Carolina's 0.57% property tax rate is well below the national average. The state offers a 4% assessment ratio for owner-occupied primary residences (vs. 6% for investment properties), significantly reducing the tax bill. South Carolina's income tax tops out at 6.5%.
south-carolina.insuranceContent: South Carolina's coastal areas (Charleston, Myrtle Beach, Hilton Head) face significant hurricane and flood risk, with annual premiums of $2,500–$6,000+. Inland areas have more moderate rates. Budget $150–$250+/month depending on location.
south-carolina.marketContent: Charleston has become one of the most desirable cities in the Southeast, with strong demand from retirees, remote workers, and corporate relocations. Greenville has seen strong growth driven by BMW, Michelin, and a growing tech sector. Columbia (University of South Carolina) has a stable university-driven market. Much of rural South Carolina qualifies for USDA loans.

south-dakota.name: South Dakota

statePrograms.name: SDHDA First-Time Homebuyer Program
statePrograms.description: Below-market interest rate mortgages with optional down payment assistance for qualifying first-time homebuyers in South Dakota.

south-dakota.metaTitle: South Dakota Mortgage Calculator 2026 | SD Home Loan Payment Estimator
south-dakota.metaDescription: Calculate your South Dakota mortgage payment. South Dakota has no state income tax and moderate property taxes (1.14%). Sioux Falls is one of the fastest-growing cities in the Midwest. Free SD calculator 2026.
south-dakota.intro: South Dakota has no state income tax and no state sales tax on most items, making it one of the most tax-friendly states in the nation. Sioux Falls has emerged as one of the fastest-growing cities in the Midwest, driven by financial services, healthcare, and a business-friendly environment.
south-dakota.taxContent: South Dakota has no state income tax — a significant financial advantage. Property taxes average 1.14%, close to the national average. The state offers a Homestead Exemption for qualifying seniors and disabled individuals. The combination of no income tax and moderate property taxes makes South Dakota very tax-competitive.
south-dakota.insuranceContent: South Dakota faces tornado risk, particularly in the eastern part of the state. Severe thunderstorms and hail are common. Annual insurance averages $1,200–$1,600. Budget $100–$135/month.
south-dakota.marketContent: Sioux Falls has been one of the fastest-growing cities in the Midwest, driven by financial services (Citibank, Wells Fargo), healthcare, and a business-friendly environment. Rapid City (near Mount Rushmore and the Black Hills) has a stable tourism and military-influenced market. Much of rural South Dakota qualifies for USDA loans.

tennessee.name: Tennessee

statePrograms.name: THDA Great Choice Home Loan
statePrograms.description: Below-market interest rate mortgages with optional down payment assistance for qualifying first-time homebuyers in Tennessee.

tennessee.metaTitle: Tennessee Mortgage Calculator 2026 | TN Home Loan Payment Estimator
tennessee.metaDescription: Calculate your Tennessee mortgage payment. Tennessee has no income tax and low property taxes (0.71%). Nashville market has cooled from 2022 peak. Free TN calculator 2026.
tennessee.intro: Tennessee has no state income tax on wages, low property taxes (0.71%), and no inheritance tax — a combination that has made it one of the most popular destination states for corporate relocations and remote workers. Nashville experienced dramatic price appreciation before cooling from its 2022 peak.
tennessee.taxContent: Tennessee has no state income tax on wages (the Hall Tax on investment income was eliminated in 2021). Property taxes average 0.71%, well below the national average. The state offers a Homestead Exemption for qualifying homeowners. Tennessee's overall tax burden is among the lowest in the nation.
tennessee.insuranceContent: Tennessee faces tornado risk, particularly in the western part of the state. Severe thunderstorms and occasional flooding are also concerns. Annual insurance averages $1,400–$2,000. Budget $120–$170/month.
tennessee.marketContent: Nashville experienced extraordinary appreciation during 2020–2022 driven by corporate relocations (Oracle, Amazon, AllianceBernstein) and in-migration from high-tax states. The market has since cooled with more inventory. Memphis offers very affordable prices with a strong logistics economy. Knoxville and Chattanooga offer affordable alternatives with growing economies. Much of rural Tennessee qualifies for USDA loans.

texas.name: Texas

statePrograms.name: My First Texas Home
statePrograms.description: 30-year fixed-rate mortgage with down payment and closing cost assistance up to 5% of the loan amount.

texas.metaTitle: Texas Mortgage Calculator 2026 | TX Home Loan Payment Estimator
texas.metaDescription: Calculate your Texas mortgage payment. Texas has no income tax but high property taxes (avg 1.80%). Homestead Exemption available. Community property state. Free TX calculator 2026.
texas.intro: Texas has no state income tax, which meaningfully increases purchasing power, but property taxes averaging 1.80% are among the highest in the nation. On a $310,000 home, property taxes add approximately $465 per month. Texas is a community property state, and the Homestead Exemption provides significant tax relief for primary residents.
texas.taxContent: Texas has no state income tax. However, property taxes average 1.80% — on a $310,000 home, about $465/month. The Homestead Exemption reduces the school district taxable value by $100,000 for primary residences, saving approximately $1,500–$2,000/year. Texas is a community property state.
texas.insuranceContent: Texas faces hurricane risk along the Gulf Coast (Houston, Corpus Christi, Galveston) and severe weather statewide. Annual insurance averages $2,000–$3,500 for most properties, with coastal areas seeing $4,000–$8,000+. Hail damage is extremely common. Budget $150–$300+/month depending on location.
texas.marketContent: Texas has five major metros: Houston (energy, healthcare, diversity), Dallas-Fort Worth (tech, finance, corporate HQs), San Antonio (military, healthcare, tourism), Austin (tech, government, university), and Fort Worth (logistics, manufacturing). All have cooled from 2022 peaks with more inventory. Texas has significant USDA-eligible rural areas and strong VA loan demand from its many military installations.

utah.name: Utah

statePrograms.name: Utah Housing FirstHome Loan
statePrograms.description: Below-market interest rate mortgages with optional down payment assistance for qualifying first-time homebuyers in Utah.

utah.metaTitle: Utah Mortgage Calculator 2026 | UT Home Loan Payment Estimator
utah.metaDescription: Calculate your Utah mortgage payment. Utah median home price ~$500K, very low property taxes (0.58%). Salt Lake City market has cooled from 2022 peak. Free UT calculator 2026.
utah.intro: Utah experienced one of the most dramatic housing booms in the nation during 2020–2022, driven by tech sector growth (the 'Silicon Slopes'), in-migration from California, and a young, growing population. The market has since cooled from its peak. Utah's very low property tax rate (0.58%) partially offsets the high purchase prices.
utah.taxContent: Utah's 0.58% property tax rate is one of the lowest in the nation. On a $500,000 home, expect ~$242/month in taxes. Utah's income tax is a flat 4.65%. The combination of low property taxes and a moderate flat income tax makes Utah's overall tax burden quite favorable.
utah.insuranceContent: Utah faces wildfire risk, particularly in the Wasatch Front foothills and rural areas. The state also faces earthquake risk from the Wasatch Fault. Standard insurance averages $900–$1,400/year. Budget $75–$120/month.
utah.marketContent: The Wasatch Front (Salt Lake City, Provo, Ogden) is the center of Utah's housing market, driven by the 'Silicon Slopes' tech corridor (Adobe, Qualtrics, Domo, and numerous startups). The market has cooled from its 2022 peak with more inventory. Park City is a premium ski resort market. St. George in southern Utah has seen strong growth driven by retirees and remote workers.

vermont.name: Vermont

statePrograms.name: VHFA Move Program
statePrograms.description: Below-market interest rate mortgages with optional down payment assistance for qualifying homebuyers in Vermont.

vermont.metaTitle: Vermont Mortgage Calculator 2026 | VT Home Loan Payment Estimator
vermont.metaDescription: Calculate your Vermont mortgage payment. Vermont median home price ~$380K with high property taxes (1.90%). Burlington market has seen strong appreciation. Free VT calculator 2026.
vermont.intro: Vermont has seen significant housing demand from remote workers and lifestyle buyers attracted to its natural beauty and quality of life. The state has high property taxes (1.90%) and high income taxes, but Burlington has emerged as a surprisingly dynamic small city with a strong tech and healthcare sector.
vermont.taxContent: Vermont's 1.90% property tax rate is above the national average. The state offers a Homestead Declaration that provides some property tax relief for primary residences. Vermont's income tax tops out at 8.75% for high earners. The combined tax burden is significant.
vermont.insuranceContent: Vermont's insurance costs are moderate at $900–$1,300/year. The state faces winter storm risk including heavy snowfall, ice storms, and nor'easters. Flooding is a concern in some areas — Tropical Storm Irene (2011) caused significant damage. Budget $75–$110/month.
vermont.marketContent: Burlington has seen strong appreciation driven by the University of Vermont, a growing tech sector, and remote workers from Boston and New York. The state's tight housing inventory (one of the lowest per capita in the nation) has kept prices elevated. Vermont has significant USDA-eligible rural areas. Ski resort communities (Stowe, Killington) are premium second-home markets.

virginia.name: Virginia

statePrograms.name: VHDA Down Payment Assistance Grant
statePrograms.description: Down payment assistance grant of up to 2.5% of the purchase price for qualifying first-time homebuyers in Virginia.

virginia.metaTitle: Virginia Mortgage Calculator 2026 | VA Home Loan Payment Estimator
virginia.metaDescription: Calculate your Virginia mortgage payment. Virginia median home price ~$390K, low property taxes (0.82%), high conforming loan limits ($1,149,825). Strong military and federal employment base. Free VA calculator 2026.
virginia.intro: Virginia's housing market is anchored by the Northern Virginia (NoVA) DC suburbs, which have some of the highest home prices and strongest demand in the Mid-Atlantic, driven by federal government employment, defense contractors, and Amazon's HQ2. The state has high conforming loan limits ($1,149,825) in many counties and one of the largest military populations in the nation.
virginia.taxContent: Virginia's 0.82% property tax rate is below the national average. Property taxes vary significantly by locality — Northern Virginia localities have higher rates than rural areas. Virginia's income tax tops out at 5.75%. The state has no statewide homestead exemption, but some localities offer relief programs.
virginia.insuranceContent: Virginia's coastal areas (Virginia Beach, Norfolk, Hampton Roads) face hurricane and flood risk. Flood insurance is required in FEMA flood zones. Inland areas have moderate insurance costs. Annual insurance averages $1,100–$1,600. Budget $90–$135/month.
virginia.marketContent: Northern Virginia (Arlington, Alexandria, Fairfax, Loudoun counties) has some of the highest prices in the Mid-Atlantic, driven by federal employment, defense contractors, and Amazon's HQ2 in Arlington. Richmond has seen strong revitalization and appreciation. Hampton Roads (Virginia Beach, Norfolk, Chesapeake) has a large military presence supporting VA loan demand. Virginia's $1,149,825 conforming limit is essential for NoVA buyers.

washington.name: Washington

statePrograms.name: WSHFC Home Advantage Program
statePrograms.description: Below-market interest rate mortgages with optional down payment assistance for qualifying homebuyers in Washington.

washington.metaTitle: Washington Mortgage Calculator 2026 | WA Home Loan Payment Estimator
washington.metaDescription: Calculate your Washington mortgage payment. Washington has no income tax and high conforming loan limits ($1,149,825). Seattle market has cooled from 2022 peak. Community property state. Free WA calculator 2026.
washington.intro: Washington State has no state income tax, which is a significant financial advantage for residents, particularly those working in the high-paying tech sector (Amazon, Microsoft, Boeing). The state has high conforming loan limits ($1,149,825) reflecting elevated Seattle-area prices. Washington is a community property state.
washington.taxContent: Washington has no state income tax — a major advantage for high earners in the tech sector. Property taxes average 0.98%, close to the national average. Washington is a community property state. The state does have a capital gains tax (7%) on gains over $250,000, which can affect real estate transactions.
washington.insuranceContent: Washington faces significant earthquake risk from the Cascadia Subduction Zone — separate earthquake insurance is strongly recommended and averages $800–$2,500/year. Wildfire risk is significant in eastern Washington. Standard insurance averages $900–$1,400/year. Budget $900–$1,400/year plus earthquake coverage.
washington.marketContent: Seattle has cooled significantly from its 2022 peak, with more inventory and longer days on market. Bellevue and the Eastside (Redmond, Kirkland) remain premium markets driven by Microsoft and Amazon. Tacoma offers more affordable options with Seattle commuting access. Spokane has seen strong growth driven by remote workers. Washington's $1,149,825 conforming limit is essential for Seattle metro buyers.

west-virginia.name: West Virginia

statePrograms.name: WVHDF Homeownership Program
statePrograms.description: Below-market interest rate mortgages with optional down payment assistance for qualifying first-time homebuyers in West Virginia.

west-virginia.metaTitle: West Virginia Mortgage Calculator 2026 | WV Home Loan Payment Estimator
west-virginia.metaDescription: Calculate your West Virginia mortgage payment. West Virginia has the lowest median home prices in the continental U.S. (~$155K) with low property taxes (0.59%). Free WV calculator 2026.
west-virginia.intro: West Virginia has the lowest median home prices in the continental United States at around $155,000, making it one of the most accessible states for first-time homebuyers on a tight budget. The state's low property taxes (0.59%) and low cost of living further enhance affordability.
west-virginia.taxContent: West Virginia's 0.59% property tax rate is well below the national average. The state offers a Homestead Exemption for qualifying seniors. West Virginia's income tax has been significantly reformed, moving toward a flat rate of 4.82% (reduced from higher rates). The overall tax burden is quite low.
west-virginia.insuranceContent: West Virginia faces flooding risk, particularly in the mountainous eastern counties and along river valleys. The 2016 flooding in southern West Virginia caused significant damage. Annual insurance averages $1,000–$1,400. Budget $85–$120/month.
west-virginia.marketContent: West Virginia's housing market is among the most affordable in the nation. Morgantown (West Virginia University) has the most dynamic market in the state. Charleston (state capital) and Huntington offer affordable options. The state has extensive USDA-eligible rural areas, making zero-down homeownership widely accessible. Remote work has brought some new buyers to the state's scenic mountain communities.

wisconsin.name: Wisconsin

statePrograms.name: WHEDA Advantage Conventional
statePrograms.agency: Wisconsin Housing and Economic Development Authority (WHEDA)
statePrograms.description: Below-market interest rate mortgages with optional down payment assistance for qualifying first-time homebuyers in Wisconsin.

wisconsin.metaTitle: Wisconsin Mortgage Calculator 2026 | WI Home Loan Payment Estimator
wisconsin.metaDescription: Calculate your Wisconsin mortgage payment. Wisconsin median home price ~$280K but above-average property taxes (1.85%). Madison is one of the Midwest's most dynamic cities. Free WI calculator 2026.
wisconsin.intro: Wisconsin offers affordable home prices but above-average property taxes (1.85%), which is an important factor in calculating your total monthly payment. Madison has emerged as one of the most dynamic cities in the Midwest, driven by the University of Wisconsin, a growing tech sector, and state government employment. Wisconsin is a community property state.
wisconsin.taxContent: Wisconsin's 1.85% property tax rate is above the national average — on a $280,000 home, about $432/month. The state offers a Homestead Credit for qualifying lower-income homeowners. Wisconsin's income tax tops out at 7.65%. Wisconsin is a community property state, meaning assets acquired during marriage are generally owned equally by both spouses.
wisconsin.insuranceContent: Wisconsin faces winter storm risk including heavy snowfall, ice storms, and extreme cold. Severe thunderstorms and occasional tornadoes affect the state. Annual insurance averages $1,100–$1,500. Budget $90–$130/month.
wisconsin.marketContent: Madison has been one of the fastest-growing Midwest cities, driven by the University of Wisconsin, Epic Systems (healthcare IT), and state government. Milwaukee offers very affordable prices with ongoing revitalization in neighborhoods like the Third Ward and Bay View. Green Bay has a stable manufacturing and healthcare economy. Much of rural Wisconsin qualifies for USDA loans.

wyoming.name: Wyoming

statePrograms.name: WCDA Advantage Program
statePrograms.description: Below-market interest rate mortgages with optional down payment assistance for qualifying first-time homebuyers in Wyoming.

wyoming.metaTitle: Wyoming Mortgage Calculator 2026 | WY Home Loan Payment Estimator
wyoming.metaDescription: Calculate your Wyoming mortgage payment. Wyoming has no income tax, no corporate tax, and low property taxes (0.61%). One of the most tax-friendly states in the nation. Free WY calculator 2026.
wyoming.intro: Wyoming has no state income tax, no corporate tax, and low property taxes (0.61%), making it one of the most tax-friendly states in the nation. The state's economy is driven by energy (oil, gas, coal) and tourism (Yellowstone, Grand Teton), creating distinct regional housing markets.
wyoming.taxContent: Wyoming has no state income tax and no corporate tax. Property taxes average 0.61%, well below the national average. On a $330,000 home, expect ~$168/month in taxes. Wyoming's overall tax burden is among the lowest in the nation, funded primarily by mineral extraction revenues.
wyoming.insuranceContent: Wyoming faces winter storm risk including heavy snowfall, blizzards, and extreme cold. Wildfire risk is present in forested areas. Annual insurance averages $900–$1,300. Budget $75–$110/month.
wyoming.marketContent: Wyoming's housing market is driven by energy sector employment in Casper and Gillette, government employment in Cheyenne, and the University of Wyoming in Laramie. Jackson Hole is one of the most expensive resort markets in the nation, driven by second-home demand from ultra-high-net-worth buyers. Much of rural Wyoming qualifies for USDA loans.
